entry.title: Re: Special character # -issue in the file generated
entry.content: hi andrew , Thanks , but we have tried few things and still the issue remains. We have added just the content modifier to the preexisting flow, where we set the header as Charset and value 'UTF-8' and we tested - still seeing # in SAP. We wrote a groovy to replace '\n' with '' , still same result. We do not see the # in IS trace mode nor in the file at AL11, it only appears once the file is being processed at SAP. Any other suggestions which we can try?

entry.title: Is there way enable and disable tile and catalog through api in SAP workzone
entry.content:
Hi Team,

There is requirement to enable or disable  tile and catalog on certain user action in capm application.

is there any way we can control the visibility of tile on work zone launchpage ?

for some user i want to display or assign catalog if he qualify the condition.

Regards

Ratnesh

Ratnesh

entry.title: Org Chart Navigation: Enable Users to Drill Down and Return to Previous Levels
entry.content:
Hi Community,

In the org chart, users should be able to navigate both up and down levels in a consistent manner. Currently, when clicking on an employee to move up one level, the org chart displays the upper level. However, if users wish to return to the previous level or hide the upper-level view, this functionality is not available.

Could we enhance the org chart navigation so that users can easily drill down to lower levels and also return to higher levels without difficulty?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between NB and RV document typ...
entry.content: Thanks @mohdsap. Should RV is used only to convert RV PR to contract because for RV there is no contract agreement field available at item level

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Business Application Studio – August 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Welcome to the August edition of our Product Updates for SAP Business Application Studio! We are excited to share with you the latest updates, enhancements, and new features that are now available.

 

HDI Container Content Management

The SAP HANA tools in SAP Business Application Studio offer a new management UI for HDI container content. The HDI container content manager allows deploying, undeploying, and reading content from a bound HDI container service from and to the current dev space.

The HDI container content manager can be started via the command palette command “SAP HANA: Start HDI Container Content Manager”. On the first screen a user can select the type of operation: deploy, undeploy, or recover.

In the next screen a database module bound to an HDI container service must be selected. Once a valid module has been selected the state of the dev space is compared to the state of the database. The results are shown in a table:

Depending on the chosen mode files can be deployed, undeployed, or recovered into the current dev space. The mode can be changed at any time via the “Mode” dropdown menu.

Artifact recovery can, for example, be used to implement firefighter scenarios in production systems where the current state of the database is unclear or difficult to get. The state of the production container can easily be recovered into a dev space and analyzed or debugged by a firefighter user.

Undeployment of individual files can be helpful when changing stacked database objects. Via the undeployment operation the entire hierarchy of objects can be recursively undeployed from the database and can be rebuilt from the bottom which can be difficult because of revalidation errors if objects are changed incompatibly.

 

HANA Calculation Views in the Storyboard

Calculation Views allow the definition of complex scenarios based on data available in an SAP HANA database. When working in a full stack application, you can now view the SAP HANA calculation views used in your project from the storyboard. The storyboard shows the connection between the view and the data model on which your service is based. Find here further information about the Storyboard and Project Explorer.

 

Release of UI5 Tooling 4.0 

This new release is packed with key enhancements, primarily focusing on enabling seamless UI5 2.x compatibility for your projects. Additionally, we've introduced a new feature for automatically generating locale information in the ‘manifest.json’, along with important housekeeping updates. We're committed to minimizing upgrade efforts for our developers. Projects currently using UI5 Tooling 3.x are generally expected to seamlessly transition to UI5 Tooling 4.0. Ready to dive into all the details? Head over to this blog post to explore the full scope of new features, improvements, and upgrade guidance: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/ui5-tooling-4-0/ba-p/13769578

 

Updated Java version in SAP Business Application Studio

To keep SAP Business Application Studio updated with industry standards we want to be aligned with the most recent Java LTS and formally support it. Thus, the default Java version of SAP Business Application Studio was updated to Java 21. All Java extensions were also updated to the latest version. As a Java developer you have the option to choose the Java version, using the ASDF version manager. This allows you to choose the Java version best suited for your purposes.

 

For further information on new features, please have a look at the “What’s new” information for SAP Business Application Studio and check our roadmap. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: Re: My geekiest hobby : Learning, Designing, sharing knowledge and Killing Business pain points.
entry.content: Mr. Pavan your hard work and dedication are inspiring. I'm confident that you'll continue to achieve great things.

entry.title: Open documents exclude all orders with date 01-01 (all years)
entry.content:
Hi,

I am working on a query of the open customer orders.

I want this query not to show the orders with date DAY 01 MONTH 01. So all orders from January 1 (every year) should not be visible.

I have tried the options below. These work, but do not show all orders that start with DAY 01. So all orders from February 1, March 1, and April 1 are not visible. I only want all the orders from 1 january not to be visible.

 

 

SELECT 
T0.[DocNum],
T1.[Quantity],
T1.[ItemCode],
T1.[Dscription],
T0.[DocDate],
T0.[CardCode], 
T0.[CardName]

FROM 
ORDR T0  
INNER JOIN RDR1 T1 ON T0.[DocEntry] = T1.[DocEntry] 

WHERE 
T0.[CardCode] =[%0] AND
T0.[DocDate] <=[%1] AND
T1.[LineStatus]= 'O' AND
month(T0.[DocDate]) <> '1' and day(T0.[DocDate]) <> '1'

ORDER BY
T1.[ItemCode]

 

 

 

 

 

SELECT 
T0.[DocNum],
T1.[Quantity],
T1.[ItemCode],
T1.[Dscription],
T0.[DocDate],
T0.[CardCode], 
T0.[CardName]

FROM 
ORDR T0  
INNER JOIN RDR1 T1 ON T0.[DocEntry] = T1.[DocEntry] 

WHERE 
T0.[CardCode] =[%0] AND
T0.[DocDate] <=[%1] AND
T1.[LineStatus]= 'O' AND
Datepart(DD,T0.DocDate) <> '01' And DatePart(MM,T0.DocDate) <> '01'

ORDER BY
T1.[ItemCode]

entry.content: Very supportive and hard working

entry.title: In Position, visibility of Higher-Level Positions field should be based on Cluster/Business Unit
entry.content:
Hi Community,

In the "Higher Level Position" field for a position, we need to ensure that only positions relevant to the specific cluster or Business Unit (BU) are visible. Users should not be able to see positions from other clusters or BUs.

For example, when creating a position for a particular BU, the "Higher Level Position" field should only display positions related to that specific BU, excluding positions from other BUs.

Please let us know the feasibility of this restriction.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP EWM RF with Screen Personas - Use WebGUI Instead of MobGUI
entry.content:
Hello Community,

In addition to SAP S/4 Hana 2022, we also have a system with SAP S/4 Hana 2020 Release. As you know, the MOBGUI service is only available from the 2021 release.
However, if I now simply open the transaction via the WebGUI instead of the MOBGUI service, then hide the unnecessary standard elements (header and footer), it looks visually identical to the MobGUI version. I have tested the whole thing briefly and it works so far.
Now my question is, are there any disadvantages if I use the WebGUI instead of the MobGUI? Is the performance worse? As I understand it, the MobGUI is actually the WebGUI without header and footer.

MobGUI

 

 

WebGUI

Is there anyone who uses this?

I am grateful for any feedback.

Best regards Simon

entry.title: Re: Searching for a Fixed Asset with Known Master Data at...
entry.content: Hello, You can try this: go to AR01 and then Menu Bar -> Edit -> Dynamic Selections. In the Origin data you can find 'Serial Number'. Selecting this parameter will enable serial number on the search screen.

entry.title: Re: My geekiest hobby : Learning, Designing, sharing knowledge and Killing Business pain points.
entry.content: Always eager to learn new technologies @P1_Marotkar

entry.title: Re: Visual Studio 2022 & Crystal Reports 2008
entry.content:
Always use the latest SP available, right now it's SP 36, they are full builds so to take advantage of the latest supported platforms and security updates you need to update each time, roughly twice a year...

You can get it from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

To get Crystal Reports 2020 to verify your reports and data sources you can update Crystal Reports designer from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CRYSTAL_REPORTS

The reason to update your RPT files is because the Reports do keep the database info within the file and that can cause connectivity issue with updated releases.

Note: the only way to update the Report version is to edit the reports in CR Designer or CR for VS Embedded Designer.

entry.title: Re: SD: Use price from certain MM-info-record as sales price
entry.content:
Hello, 

If you have a MM (Purchasing) Condition Record, then it is possible to copy it to SD Condition Type in Pricing. 

In SD Condition Type, (under Master Data) there is field for Reference Condition Type and Reference  Application. Here you can mention Purchasing Condition Type and Ref. Application as M. 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Can I have SAP learning portal in dark mode?
entry.content: In my Fiori settings I was able to enable white text on a black background under Accessibility.  That doesn't fix the white screen everywhere (Learning Admin screens still are occasionally white) but seems to do the trick in the Learning Portal.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content:
Hello,

Here is my solution for Task 4 🎉:

  1. Object page displaying the details of the selected record including Criticality representation of the Status field

Thank you! 😊

entry.title: Re: How do I add a row above the first data row in a SAC...
entry.content:
It is not possible with "standard functions" because those only insert "below", as already said.

But there is a trick around:

you could use the Cross Calculations ( depending if your Measures are in rows or columns) for example if your Measures are in rows, then use Story Calculations.

Steps:

1. Insert a "Dummy Caluculation", for example CM_DUMMY - with Calculation   Formula :  0. (e.g. from Cross Calculations, Restricted Measure, whatever) - arrange the sort order of the new measure (it goes to bottom when you create it, change it to first place in sort order)

2. enter an empty row below the Dummy Measure / Dimension (probably Single)

3. Hide the Dummy Measure again. the empty row stays

4. Rename / Categorize use the new row.

entry.title: Re: Get logged in user data in SAP Work Zone

entry.content:
Hi , 

we are in S/4HANA (BW 7.56) and using CDS based queries.

I have implemented all RRI relevant snotes in the system.

And defined the RRI for a CDS based query on RSBBS transaction.  

and mapped the fields between 2 reports. 

But after RRI setting I'm not able to see jump option on RSRT. I have also tried same in AFO and SAC, also not able to see it there. 

Do you know if I miss anything. Or is it not possible to set up an RRI  between 2 CDS queries ? 

Thanks 

Erkan

Is there anyone who could give me some hints how I could add CO_BELNR to RPSCO in the data view I am going to build?

#SAP PS, #RPSCO

entry.title: Release procedure configuration using standard workflow for multiple users
entry.content:
SAP MM Experts,

We have a scenario to configure release strategy for 50 plants. Each plant will have different set of approvers whose user id we need to maintain in workflow and so they can release PR/PO in Fiori.

If it was same approvers say same buyer and purchase manager for all the 50 plants, we could have created 1 release group, 2 release code (one for buyer and one for manager), create 1 release strategy whose classification will include all these 50 plants and maintain SAP user id of these 2 users for the release group and code combination in workflow setting.

However, for different users as approvers for each plant, which one of below option is feasible and advisable?

Option 1: To create 50 different release codes (1 for each approver), assign to one release group or vice versa (create 50 groups (1 for each plant) and 2 release codes) and then create 50 different release strategy i.e., each plant will have 1 plant as characteristic value, so 50 release group and release code combination can be maintained in workflow with their SAP user id so for each plant the respective approver receives the workflow notification.

Option 2: Use the customer exits. (User exit EXIT_SAPLEBNF_005 in enhancment M06E0005 is available for custom agent determination in Purchase Orders and user exit EXIT_SAPLEBNF_001 (M06B0001) for Purchase Requisitions.)

Through this we can assign both release codes to 1 release grp, we create 1 release strategy for all plants, create custom table with set of approvers for each plant and use customer exits to determine the approvers.

Is there any other way we can fulfill this requirement through standard functionality or using customer exit is feasible.Do we need to do some workflow configuration after creating custom table for approver and using customer exit to determine approvers?

I appreciate your feedback.

Thanks.

Remember we are not passing Site Groups.

Bapi Returns the error “ Site group is incomplete: Site group number or class type missing”

Pls advise.

It’s always mandatory to pass site group? What if we don’t have site group and pass only site.

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: How to remove Shipping notification form MD04 and sto...
entry.content:
Is this an Advanced Shipping Notification , coming from Vendor ? Does the customer don't want to show in MD04 or in MRP itself? 

If it is only for MD04 then you can use the Selection filter - in which you can remove the shipping notification and cater as per the customer requirement.

Configuration Menu Path:Material Requirements Planning>Evaluation>Filter>Define Selection Rules

This should help you .

Br

Satheeshkumar D

Br,

Satheeshkumar

entry.title: Follow tutorial to Develop a CAP-based application using GenAI/RAG but subscription failed
entry.content:
Hi All,

I follow this scenario Develop a CAP-based (multitenant) application using GenAI and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and execute successfully all steps for multitenant version with CF runtime until step "Create an API Service Instance (Multitenant only)". In this step, I'm failing with the subscription of the API Service Broker. The service isn't in the service list. I double checked the created credentials for the step "Post-Deployment Actions" (I used my SAP user for the aisaas-broker-credentials) but I can't find a mistake.

What I observed is that the "check connection" for the created destination in step "Setup the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core" shows the message:
Connection to "PROVIDER_AI_CORE_DESTINATION_HUB" established. Response returned: "404: Not Found"

Any ideas what can i verify or change?

Thanks in advanced
Bernd

entry.title: Re: How replenishment lead time is getting calculated
entry.content:
Hi,

can you please check what was your scheduling parameter during the MRP live run, please use the indicator 1 and check it back !

entry.title: Importance of receiving staging area and STADET_IN transaction in EWM
entry.content:
Introduction:

In the realm of warehouse management, staging areas play a pivotal role in optimizing the receipt and processing of goods. Effective staging areas ensure that products are managed efficiently, reducing errors, and improving overall operational efficiency. In this blog, I will explore the importance of staging areas within a warehouse and how SAP EWM as a WMS can significantly enhance control over inbound deliveries and guide goods to their designated receiving areas.

The Importance of Different Staging Areas in Warehouse Layouts

In designing an efficient warehouse layout, incorporating various staging areas is key for optimizing operations and ensuring a smooth workflow. Each staging area is tailored to handle specific types of inventory and processing needs.

Bulk Items Staging Area

For bulk items, warehouses often include a staging area with larger doors and ample space. This dedicated area facilitates the efficient receipt and storage of large quantities of goods. By having a separate space for bulk items, warehouses can streamline the unloading process and manage these items more effectively, ensuring that they are stored and handled appropriately.

Perishables and Hazardous Materials

Perishables and hazardous materials require specialized handling due to their unique characteristics. Separate receiving areas for these types of goods ensure they are stored under optimal conditions and comply with safety regulations. For perishables, this might include refrigeration or controlled environments, while hazardous materials need specific safety protocols and containment measures to prevent accidents and ensure regulatory compliance.

Intensive Quality Inspection and Counting

Some products necessitate detailed quality inspections and accurate counting upon arrival. Dedicated receiving areas for these items allow for focused attention and thorough examination. This separation helps ensure that products meet quality standards and are correctly accounted for before they move further into the warehouse or distribution process.

High-Value Items

Tracking and managing high-value items often require a higher level of security and precision. By allocating separate receiving areas for these goods, warehouses can enhance inventory control, reduce the risk of loss or theft, and ensure that these valuable items are handled with the necessary care.

Safety and Regulatory Compliance

Certain products, such as chemicals and pharmaceuticals, come with stringent safety regulations. Having designated receiving areas for these items ensures adherence to safety standards and regulations. This separation helps in managing the special handling requirements and minimizes the risk of contamination or accidents, ensuring a safe working environment.

By integrating different staging areas into warehouse layouts, businesses can improve operational efficiency, enhance safety, and ensure effective inventory management. Each area is designed to handle specific types of goods, facilitating a more organized and streamlined warehouse operation.

How STADET_IN helps

In the inbound delivery process, determining the goods movement bin (GMBIN) is crucial for smooth and error-free inbound delivery. The system identifies the GM bin based on the warehouse process type (source storage type and source bin maintained in WPT settings)

However, if there is a business need to receive products from different suppliers in distinct areas, even if the WPT remains the same, the system should accommodate these requirements. For example:

Product A from Supplier X should be received in GR AREA 1, which is set up for mixed product reception and requires deconsolidation.

Product A from Supplier Y should be received in GR AREA 2, which is designated for fully packed pallets and does not require deconsolidation.

To propose the correct staging area, SAP EWM offers a functionality(transaction) called /SCWM/STADET_IN. This transaction allows you to maintain the necessary details, enabling the system to suggest the appropriate staging area.

Based on the supplier (ship-from), we can assign different staging areas and doors for receiving, even when the same WPT is in use.

However, don’t forget to maintain the access sequence for this setup. Use transaction /SCWM/STADET_ASS to configure it accordingly.

We will test this in the system using two different inbound deliveries for the same material, each from a different supplier.

Inbound delivery- 180000087

Inbound delivery- 180000088

Now, let’s explore another example of how to determine the staging area using the staging area/door determination group, which is typically maintained in each product master. For instance, heavy products might require different staging doors (larger doors), while lightweight materials can be accommodated through standard doors and receiving areas.

First, maintain the staging area/door determination group in the product master,

/SCWM/MAT1

We can configure this value in the SPRO,

Now maintain the staging area/door determination group in the /SCWM/STADET_IN

First, assign the appropriate staging area/door determination group to the product master. Then, maintain this group in the STADET_IN transaction.

Material

Description

Staging area/door determination group

TG101

Bulk trading goods

Z003

TG102

Lightweight trading goods

Z001

Also, maintain the /SCWM/STADET_ASS

Check the below screenshot,

Please note that all testing and screenshots are taken from the S4HANA 2023 demo system. This demo environment is used to simulate real-world scenarios and validate configurations. As such, the screenshots and results presented are based on this specific system version and may differ from other environments or versions.

entry.title: Material Master - Mass changes in S/4 Hana Cloud
entry.content:
Dear all,

My company plans on installing Master Data Governance next year. However, by the time we get the hand on the module, we'll have to deal with mass changes (no creation, only modification) in Material Master for all type of data in the opened views.

As such, do you know if S/4 Hana gives us such possiblity (native options) ? Correct me if I'm wrong about the existing options :

- MM17 still exists in S/4 Hana but requires that that all data for the materials and organizational levels you select should be the same

- Does LTMC gives you the possiblity to do mass changes on all type of data ?

If you have any other recommendation, I'll be more than happy to read it.

Yours sincerely,

entry.title: Re: New trial capability for SAP Application Interface Framework
entry.content:
@VenuRavipati you need to install the add-on AIFGEN to enable the license in your SAP system, see https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2288871

Alex

entry.title: Re: sapmmc is empty after restarting the PC
entry.content: Hi, I am facing the same issue. can you please help to do this process. So where should I navigate and do this unchecking and checking. Please help me out with this. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Blank dates at intermediate location in FU document v...
entry.content:
Hello Kumar,

Indeed this is not handled in standard ... Had to implement several years ago in specific during FU integration. At this time we went through a process controller. 

I would rather recommend to use the badi /SCMTMS/FU_STAGE_MODIFY which was not existing in 2017.

Hope it helps.

Adrien Manier

entry.title: Re: Is there way enable and disable tile and catalog through api in SAP workzone
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites, it's reserved to issues with SAP Community, Support and so on. Better post to Technology Q&A (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions). Hopefully, an admin would fix it for you.

entry.title: Re: Open documents exclude all orders with date 01-01 (al...
entry.content: The contrary of 'A and B' is 'not A or not B' 😎

entry.title: Re: ALTERNATIVE RESOURCE
entry.content:
Hi,

your can classify the workcenters and send all over in order to get them all shown as alternative modes (resources) in the PDS.

2469590 Alternative resources from work center classification not updated in the PDS in APO (Product Data Structure)

In order to filter by the products you can use selecons in order to select the needed resources.

 

Or you can try to use the ref. char setup as given in note 610873 - SCM: Using reference characteristics in PP/DS-PDS

Regards,

Sushmita

entry.title: Unlocking the Future with SAP: A Path to Success
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business world, staying ahead of the curve is paramount. This is where SAP (Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing) comes into play. SAP has not only revolutionized the way businesses operate but has also created a niche for itself in the technology domain. Looking ahead, the scope of SAP in the future is promising and limitless.

1. Growing Demand for SAP Professionals

The demand for SAP professionals is on the rise, and this trend shows no signs of slowing down. As companies continue to integrate technology into their operations, the need for skilled SAP consultants, analysts, and developers will only increase. This demand spans across various industries, including finance, healthcare, manufacturing, and more.

2. Evolving Technology Landscape

SAP is constantly evolving to keep pace with technological advancements. With the advent of cloud computing, SAP has transitioned to cloud-based solutions, providing businesses with greater flexibility and scalability. This shift ensures that SAP professionals will remain relevant and in demand as they adapt to these innovations.

3. Global Business Expansion

In an interconnected world, businesses are expanding their operations globally. SAP's global reach and its ability to provide standardized solutions make it an invaluable tool for multinational corporations. As companies continue to expand into new markets, the demand for SAP expertise will be high.

4. Data-Driven Decision Making

Data is the new gold, and SAP helps organizations mine this precious resource effectively. SAP's analytics and business intelligence capabilities enable data-driven decision-making, which is crucial for success in today's competitive landscape. SAP professionals who can harness the power of data will be in high demand.

5. Emerging Technologies Integration

SAP is at the forefront of integrating emerging technologies like artificial intelligence, machine learning, and Internet of Things (IoT) into its solutions. This integration enhances the capabilities of SAP and opens up new avenues for innovation. Professionals with expertise in these areas will be at the forefront of technological advancements.

Conclusion: SCM-Cloudbook Offers the Best SAP Courses in Pune

For those looking to seize the opportunities in the dynamic world of SAP, SCM-Cloudbook in Pune is the ideal choice. With a reputation for excellence in SAP training, SCM-Cloudbook offers a wide range of courses that cater to both beginners and experienced professionals. Their comprehensive curriculum, experienced faculty, and hands-on approach ensure that students are well-prepared for the future of SAP.

In conclusion, the scope of SAP in the future is bright and promising. It is a field that offers career stability, growth, and the opportunity to be at the forefront of technological innovations. As businesses continue to rely on SAP for their operations, the demand for skilled SAP professionals will only increase. To embark on this exciting journey, consider SCM-Cloudbook's SAP courses in Pune and unlock a world of opportunities in the realm of SAP.

entry.title: Re: How do we see the amount which would be settled for a...
entry.content:
Hi @priya_sshekhar 

You can preview the amount to be settled in WB2R_SV/WB2R_SC via execution in simulation or check mode, with detail at header or item level. 

Regards, 
Gauthier VICTOR

entry.title: Re: Table : AUSP data is not migrated using ERP direct tr...

Hello Kanako,

Please use object "Object classification (general template)" to migrate Characteristic Values.

Regards,

Pierre

Emmanuel

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
As I said, an ABAP program cannot run on a user laptop.

I guess you are not very clear in your question. If your question is not related to a user laptop, it's of course possible to run an ABAP program once an hour via a background job (SM36).

entry.title: Re: How to Set Up the Email Notification for Flexible Workflow in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a requirement to send an e-mail notification to the PO creator at the end of workflow (after final approval). Please could you support where I can change it?

Example:

I have a PO with 2 levels approval. Right now every time the PO is approved the PO creator gets two times the same e-mail notification.

The goal is to send the e-mail notification after final PO approval (after second level approves the PO).

I cannot find anywhere the customizing for it.

 

Thanks

ZK

entry.title: Re: how to set a constant attribute to strans xml transformation?
entry.content: Why would you want to define a namespace as a variable? Transformations refer 99.9% of the time to fixed namespaces. If you want a special transformation, completely dynamic, use XSLT.

entry.title: Re: Public Sector in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content: Hi Kurt.   Thanks for reading 😊.   Universal Allocations is available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Public Sector for actuals.   Allocation for planning data is still under discussion.

entry.title: Re: "SQL ALTER SYSTEM RECLAIM DATAVOLUME 120 DEFRAGMENT"
entry.content:
Ask database admin?

  • ALTER SYSTEM RECLAIM DATAVOLUME Statement (System Management) in online help 
  • ALTER SYSTEM RECLAIM DATAVOLUME Statement (System Management) in forum and support 
  • Guidelines for SAP HANA Backup and 'ALTER SYSTEM RECLAIM LOG'

entry.title: Padding is invalid and cannot be removed
entry.content:
Hi

We are using Crystal Report as a report in Visual Studio 2022.
We have purchased SAP Crystal Report 2020 for development additionally we installed
Crystal report Service Pack 36 runtime 32 and 64-bit.
we are facing an error on our server.
Error: Padding is invalid and cannot be removed.

we added a virtual application under the website for more information see the attached screenshot

entry.title: Re: Extending SAC Planning - Acessing planning data with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @HadnettSP 
the dpAgent has two functions 

  1. it provides a tunnel through a network boundary. 
  2. It provides the adapter for SDI based connections.

That is why you currently have two ways to connect to the SAC CDI endpoint. 

  1. A Remote Table, that uses the SDI (with dpAgent) functions from HANA to connect to the CDI endpoint. 
  2. A Data Flow, that uses DI functionality and therefore does not need a dpAgent. 

This will change in the future, once a SDA based CDI adapter becomes available. In that case a remote table with a direct connection is possible. 

Best 

Hannes

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest Official Rules
entry.content: Anyone that can access the SAP Community can participate. But they would be ineligible for the prize.

entry.title: How to build a RAP application to upload BAPI program in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

As classical ABAP, We have requirement to bulk data upload using excel for some transactional data using BAPIs. The same thing how we can achieve in S/4 HANA via developing RAP application only.

entry.title: Re: Course assignments from different workareas
entry.content: No, unfortunately not. The Workarea is a part of the 'identification' of the content for an Assignment, so if you move it to another Workarea you would need to create a new Assignment. (You can also create multiple Assignments for the same content in the same Workarea, which also shows why this wouldn't work.) What's the problem you're trying to solve by moving content to different Workareas?

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM RF with Screen Personas - Use WebGUI Instead...
entry.content: Hi Simon, as far as I remember messages are processed little bit differently. It is better to use same service as it be used on the mobile device. But since you do not have mobgui, it should be fine to use webgui. I do not remember any big differences.

entry.title: Re: Replication from ECC system to S/4 system using SLT
entry.content:
You need to configure table in LTRS and define for the table "Refresh behavior" as "nothing". In such case the target table will not be recreated or truncated and the new data will be simply added to existing table.

But you have risk to get an double key error if newly inserted data will have same key. If it's ok for you and you would like to overwrite existing data, then you can define "writing behavior" for the table as "Array Modify" under Performance Option parameters. Or define an own rule that somehow change the incoming records in way that they will not conflict with existing data.

entry.title: Re: Message tranform bean creating empty payload
entry.content:
Hello @durgarao1 

Was this issue fixed?

We are facing the same issue after updating to SP29. Any inputs will be much helpful!!

entry.title: Re: Requesting L-Code from SAP
entry.content: Update August 2024  - feedback received from SAP via a ticket we raised : "L-Code is not an option anymore. No new L-Code will be provided."

entry.title: SAC Add-in for Excel
entry.content:
Introduction

Hello and welcome to this blog discussing the powerful SAC Add-in tool developed by SAC to allow users to seamlessly integrate SAC with Excel in real-time. In this blog I will discuss the following points:

  • What's going on with Analysis for Office?
  • How to get the add-in
  • What is the SAC add-in for Excel?
  • Configurations and Set up

 

What's going on with Analysis for Office?

After digging through other blogs and exploring multiple websites, I finally have a clear answer to what the current situation is regarding Datasphere and AFO. All new features are now being developed only for the SAP Analytics Cloud add-in for Microsoft Office. As a result, new Datasphere Analytic Models can be used with this add-in, but not with SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (AFO). SAP has no plans to support these analytical models in SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office.

Analytical datasets can still be view in AFO as of August 2024 but will soon be deprecated.

The long and the short of it is that you should use the Analytics Cloud add-in for Microsoft Office. 

How to get the add-in

From the link below you can download and install the Microsoft add-in for Excel:  

https://appsource.microsoft.com/en-us/product/office/wa200000169?tab=overview 

 

What is the SAC add-in for Excel?

The SAC add-in for Microsoft Excel allows users to seamlessly integrate SAC with Excel in real-time. This add-in enhances SAC’s analytics and planning features by incorporating familiar Microsoft Excel functions and interface. This add-in lets users’ format, analyse, and enhance their data using the latest content from the SAC platform including content coming from Datasphere. It is also possible to add one or several tables, each based on one model, to a workbook, add or remove dimensions and measures to analyse data, create a filter to specify your analysis, add totals in rows and columns, drill into hierarchies as needed.

 

Configurations and Set up

1. Open Excel and go to SAP Analytic Cloud tab

 

2. Click on 'Sign In' and it will open a window on the right side. Paste in your SAC URL and click go

 

3. Sign in using your SAC login details

 

4. Click on ‘Add Data Source for Formulas’ and you’ll get a pop-up window like this. You’ll get a list of data sources available from/through SAC. For this example, I will connect to SAP Datasphere.

 

5. Once you click on SAP Datasphere, you’ll be asked to log into Datasphere.

6. Once you sign into Datasphere, you’ll see a list of all your spaces within Datasphere

 

7. Once you select your Space (for this example → Test Space), you can then select your view (for this example → Sales_Orders_View_GEM)

 

8. You can then create your table by adding dimension and/or measures from the builder on the right hand side.

 

Conclusion

The SAC Add-in for Excel provides an easy-to-use and powerful way to integrate SAC into your Microsoft Office workflow. By enhancing Excel’s analytic capabilities and ensuring real-time data updates, this add-in empowers users to work more efficiently with the familiar Office interface.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
Theoretically, your/one workstation/PC could run an RFC server program, so that the application server could connect to it and scheduled background tasks could communicate with the dongle.

(Abap is not the best tag for this purpose).

Don't you have any documentation or software supplied with the dongle?

entry.title: Re: Open documents exclude all orders with date 01-01 (al...

entry.title: Get SEGW project from V4 Odata service - API_WAREHOUSE_RESOURCE
entry.content:
Hi All,

I tried looking on SCN and most of the threads mention table /IWBEP/I_SBD_SV and /IWBEP/I_SBO_ET but I could not find the details. I also tried serching in SEGW using patterns but no luck.

Could you please let me know how can we get SEGW project from V4 Odata service - API_WAREHOUSE_RESOURCE

Thanks !!

entry.title: Re: Document number CO_BELNR to RPSCO
entry.content: RPSCO is a summarization table used for PS reporting, it is not a line item table.  It would be better to use COEP.

entry.title: Re: kf2 value = kf1 value of previous period
entry.content:
You seem to calculate the Projected Stock . In order to avoid cycles within your key figures (ending inventory is calculated based on beginning inventory and viceversa), you will need some helper key figures + use Cumulative Aggregation and Period Shifting formulas, as expressed here here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_INTEGRATED_BUSINESS_PLANNING/bf99e931b8d44aafb4e306ec3602cbdd/adbc9b3674544184936e4029f642fed8.html

*KF 2 = KF1 at pervious period can be achieved by KF 2 = IBP_PERIODSHIFT(KF1,1)

entry.title: Re: Report Painter - ACDOCA | ACDOCP
entry.content:
May I aks if you found a way to use the old hierarchy report in PS or you switched to App F6991?

Thanks and regards

entry.title: how to migrate partners roles of purchase contract ?
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm using S/4 Migration Cockpit to migrate Purchase Contracts

The Migration Object : "Purchase Contract" does not include Partners sheet 

Do you have any suggestion how to migrate Partners Role (to EKPA  table) for purchasing contracts ?

Thanks,

Rami

entry.title: SLT filter 30 days current date
entry.content:
Trying to filter data -30 days from current date between ECC and PERSON_NAME with SLT.  I do not see an option for this feature, is this possible?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SLT filter 30 days current date
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

To filter data based on a date you have to have a date field in the table, you can then filter by following KBA https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2581611

If you don't have a date field in the table you can create a field in LTRS -> Table Settings -> Table Structure and then use a rule assignment to populate the field. 

 

Best Regards,
SAP Support
